fields.title: Word Of Truth
fields.summary: “But he answered and said, It is written, Man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word that proceedeth out of the mouth of God.”
fields.body:
Matthew 4:4 (KJV)

“But he answered and said, It is written, Man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word that proceedeth out of the mouth of God.”

This is Jesus responding to Satan during the temptation in the wilderness.


The full temptation passage (Matthew 4:1–11)

A different verse where Jesus says “It is written”

A short teaching explanation to go with it for a slide or message.

fields.title: Q and A with BBI School of Ministry Director
fields.summary: Discover how BBI School of Ministry equips believers in deliverance and spiritual warfare through Holy Spirit-led training with global reach
fields.body:
Q: Please introduce yourself and tell us more about the BBI School of Ministry course load and how you have performed.

A: My name is Irene Mbithe, and I serve as the Director of Training at BBI School of Ministry. I oversee our trainers as we develop, test, and roll out deliverance and spiritual warfare training programs.

Everything we do is led and accelerated by the Holy Spirit. In fact, through His guidance, we developed our Level 1 Deliverance and Spiritual Warfare Training in just three weeks—a remarkable milestone.

Since September 2023, we have trained 29 students in Level 1. This foundational course establishes a strong biblical understanding of deliverance. We are now transitioning our training fully online to increase accessibility and global reach.

We are currently developing Level 2 and Level 3 programs, designed to take students deeper and equip them to operate confidently as deliverance ministers within their spheres of influence.

 

Q: What do you want to achieve with the next-level cohort at BBI School of Ministry?

A: Our vision at BBI is to see every believer in the Lord Jesus Christ set free from invisible barriers that prevent them from fulfilling their God-given purpose.

Our mission is to equip believers to live victoriously and fulfill their calling through the inspiration of the Holy Spirit.

Before enrolling in training, our students go through deliverance to break spiritual hindrances. The transformation we witness is powerful. As students understand their identity and authority in Christ, they adopt a “David versus Goliath” mindset. The freedom and confidence that follow are truly phenomenal.

 

Q: What are the main challenges facing deliverance ministry training schools, and how do you plan to address them?

A: One major challenge is that many ministries shy away from deliverance. Yet deliverance is essential for every believer. In the early church, new believers underwent deliverance before baptism—this practice has largely been lost.

Another challenge is unconscious incompetence—many believers don’t know deliverance exists, even as they struggle with invisible barriers.

Fear also plays a role. Some think ignoring the devil makes him go away. But Scripture is clear: we have an enemy. The good news is that Jesus defeated Satan at the cross.

We address these challenges through biblical teaching, equipping believers with truth, and serving as a kingdom resource ready to collaborate and learn alongside others.

 

Q: What sets BBI School of Ministry apart in deliverance training?

A: BBI School of Ministry is built upon the teachings of our founder and CEO, Pastor Nellie Shani. She has a strong biblical foundation and was mentored by The Navigators.

Our training is Holy Spirit-inspired and Bible-based—not rooted in human opinion. Scripture is our final authority.

 

Q: What are your top priorities at the school?

A: Our highest priority is alignment with the Holy Spirit. We remain anchored to our vision and mission and move only in the direction He leads.

 

Q: Tell us about a difficult situation you recently overcame concerning course delivery.

A: We initially planned to launch Level 1 Cohort 3 in February 2024. However, due to registration numbers, we postponed to March to allow more time for enrollment.

We are also trusting God for more trainers. The harvest is plentiful, but the laborers are few.

 

Q: What are your long-term goals?

A: Our long-term vision is global accessibility. We want believers anywhere in the world—Antarctica, Pakistan, Alaska, Japan, Mauritania—to access our online deliverance training and experience transformation.

 

Q: What would you like to see done differently in deliverance training generally?

A: The myth that deliverance is only for a chosen few must end. Jesus instructed His disciples to heal the sick, raise the dead, cleanse lepers, and cast out demons (Matthew 10:8).

Deliverance is part of the Great Commission. Believers must walk confidently in their identity and authority in Christ.

 

Q: How has your previous experience prepared you for this role?

Pst Dr Nellie Shani, BBI Founder and CEO

A: When I reflect on my journey, I see God’s preparation. I gave my life to Christ about 10 years ago. Salvation transformed me. As I studied Scripture, I realized we had limited God in many churches. I longed to see the God who parted the Red Sea at work today.

In 2015, I experienced deliverance through a BBI member and began formal study with various ministries. I hold a practitioner certification from Global Awakening College of Ministry in physical healing, emotional healing, and deliverance. I am pursuing Master Equippers certification and am enrolled in the Global Supernatural Discipleship (GSD) course.

Before ministry, I spent over 19 years in corporate leadership and trained as an aerospace engineer.

 

Q: How will you measure success at BBI School of Ministry?

A: Success means seeing believers understand their identity in Christ, live intimately with God, and walk victoriously in their purpose—doing great exploits for the Kingdom.

 

Q: What is the latest skill you learned, and why?

A: Emotional healing. I trained with Connect Up because emotional wounds are widespread. Inner healing and deliverance work hand in hand. We must learn to bring our pain to Jesus.

 

Q: Is there anything else you would like to share?

A: I want to honor Pastor Nellie Shani for her obedience to God’s call and for creating a platform where believers can grow and fellowship.

I honor the BBI faculty and trainers for their dedication, and our courageous students who desire to go deeper in God. Above all, I thank God for the privilege of serving in His Kingdom.

 

About BBI School of Ministry

BBI School of Ministry is a Holy Spirit-led training ground committed to equipping believers in deliverance, spiritual warfare, emotional healing, and biblical identity—preparing them to impact their communities and nations for Christ.

Click on this link https://forms.gle/G6P6QDGDiPvSPzfo6 to register in our School of Ministry.

fields.title: BBI Updates for Members
fields.summary: BBI updates its ministry policies to ensure a safe, Spirit-filled community. Learn about new Zoom prayer watch rules, financial safety, and online deliverance guidelines.
fields.body:
Strengthening Our Community: Updates & Safety Guidelines

BELOVED BBI family, we are grateful for God’s sustaining grace in our journey of faith, prayer, and transformation. Together, we cultivate a safe, loving, and accountable environment where every member is encouraged, supported, and uplifted in Christ.

 

Addressing Recent Security Concerns

Recently, our ministry platform experienced an unfortunate incident where a stranger infiltrated our space and fraudulently extorted money from a few members. While this grieves us deeply, we are responding in wisdom, not fear, implementing guidelines to strengthen our spiritual and digital walls and protect our sacred community.

 

“Like Nehemiah rebuilding Jerusalem’s walls, we are building spiritual walls of discernment, integrity, and love.”

 

New Financial Policies

To protect members and ensure transparency:

  • No one should request money during Zoom sessions.

  • If approached with personal financial requests, notify administrator Pauline immediately.

  • All legitimate needs will be processed through the BBI administration in consultation with CEO Pastor Nellie Shani.

“Our community thrives when clarity, honesty, and compassion guide every interaction.” – Pastor Nellie Shani

Zoom Meeting Guidelines

To maintain a secure and focused environment:

  1. Prayer Watch Access – Participation requires attendance at an online deliverance session. Register through the administrator before joining.

  2. Digital Security – Do not forward Zoom links, calendars, or ministry materials to outsiders.

  3. Name Identification – Use a full and recognizable name; avoid nicknames or device names (e.g., iPhone, Sam G).

  4. Corporate Prayer Culture – Actively participate in prayers; if unable to speak aloud, respond in chat.

  5. Participating in Breakthroughs – Occasionally, you may be asked to pray over your own request as others agree in prayer—this builds spiritual ownership.

Ministry Ethos: A Culture of Prayer and Growth

BBI is more than a gathering—we are a community of intercessors, deliverance ministers, prayer warriors, and teachers. Our values include:

  • Unity in the Spirit

  • Respect and honesty in communication

  • Spiritual maturity

  • Growth in prayer life

 

“We are called to be watchmen—not just for ourselves but for our brothers and sisters. Let us remain alert, prayerful, and supportive.” – Pastor Nellie

Moving Forward Together

BBI desires to maintain a safe, Spirit-filled ministry platform where members can grow, be healed, and experience divine encounters. By following these guidelines, we protect what God has entrusted to us and ensure that our community remains a haven for encouragement, accountability, and spiritual transformation.

Thank you for standing guard with us as we continue pressing forward for the glory of Jesus Christ.

fields.title: Rev Dan Ole Shani Urges Spiritual Vigilance
fields.summary: Rev Dan Ole Shani, Chairman of Breaking Barriers International, delivered a powerful message at Bread of Life Church Nairobi urging believers to endure to the end. Preaching from Matthew 24 and 2 Peter 1, he warned about love growing cold in the last days and called Christians to perseverance, repentance, and spiritual alertness.
fields.body:
REV Dan Ole Shani, Chairman of Breaking Barriers International (BBI), delivered a timely and sobering message at Bread of Life Church,Nairobi,Kenya urging believers to “endure to the end in our Christian life.”

 

Preaching from Matthew 24:12–14, Rev. Shani reminded the congregation that true discipleship is not merely about beginning the journey of faith with passion, but finishing it with perseverance.

 

“Because lawlessness will abound, the love of many will grow cold. But he who endures to the end shall be saved.” — Jesus Christ

 

He emphasized that perseverance is not optional for Christians — it is the very evidence of authentic faith.

 

Love Growing Cold in the Last Days

Drawing from 2 Timothy 3:1–5, Rev. Shani outlined 19 characteristics that signal spiritual decline in the last days. The apostle Paul warned that people would become:

 

  • Lovers of self

  • Lovers of money

  • Proud and arrogant

  • Abusive and disobedient

  • Ungrateful and unholy

  • Heartless and unforgiving

  • Slanderous and without self-control

  • Brutal and not lovers of good

  • Treacherous and reckless

  • Swollen with conceit

  • Lovers of pleasure rather than lovers of God

  • Having a form of godliness but denying its power

 

Rev. Shani cautioned that when a believer begins to exhibit these traits, it signals a dangerous drift away from intimacy with Christ.

 

“Like the proverbial frog placed in gradually heated water, many believers may not notice the spiritual decline until it is too late,” he warned.

 

He explained that spiritual deception deepens when warning signs are ignored, godly counsel is resisted, and the conviction of the Holy Spirit is continually suppressed.

 

Bread of Life Church, which serves Nairobi’s Chinese Christian community, received the message with sober reflection as Rev. Shani urged them to remain spiritually alert in what he described as a VUCA (volatile, uncertain, complex, ambiguous) world.

 

 

How to Persevere Until the End

Turning to 2 Peter 1:5–7, Rev. Shani outlined eight qualities every believer must intentionally cultivate to safeguard their faith:

 

  1. Faith – Trusting in God’s promises

  2. Virtue (Goodness) – Choosing moral excellence

  3. Knowledge – Growing in understanding of God’s Word

  4. Self-control – Mastering desires and impulses

  5. Perseverance – Standing firm under trial

  6. Godliness – Living reverently before God

  7. Brotherly kindness – Loving fellow believers

  8. Love – The crowning virtue that reflects Christ

 

“These qualities do not develop automatically,” he said. “Peter urges us to make every effort. Perseverance requires intentional pursuit, daily surrender, spiritual discipline, and reliance on the Holy Spirit.”

 

Referring to 2 Peter 1:10–11, he challenged believers to diligently confirm their calling and election.

“Enduring to the end is about clinging to Christ, abiding in His Word, and depending fully on the Spirit of God.”

 

 

A Call to Repentance and Spiritual Alertness

Rev. Shani’s message was both a warning and an invitation. He called the church to repentance, vigilance, and renewed devotion to Christ.

 

In a world marked by increasing lawlessness and spiritual compromise, he urged believers not to drift with cultural currents but to press forward with endurance fueled by love for Jesus.

 

The message concluded with Christ’s enduring promise:

“He who endures to the end shall be saved.

fields.title: Year of Miracles, Signs and Wonders | BBI 2026 Message
fields.summary: BBI’s 2026 prophetic theme: Manifesting the Kingdom Through the Word.  A year of walking in bold faith, obedience and God’s Word to release miracles, fruitfulness, and Kingdom manifestation in your life.
fields.body:
SHALOM Saints. Praise God.

 

“Now, Lord, look on their threats, and grant to Your servants that with all boldness they may speak Your word, by stretching out Your hand to heal, and that signs and wonders may be done through the name of Your holy Servant Jesus.” — Acts of the Apostles 4:29–30

 

2026: A Year of Spiritual Explosion

This is the year of:

  • A spiritual explosion

  • Miracles, signs, and wonders

  • Bold proclamation of the Word of God

  • The visible manifestation of God’s Kingdom

 

In 2026, God will reveal Himself to:

  • Those who believe

  • Those who draw closer to Him

  • Those who listen and obey His voice

“Your ears shall hear a word behind you, saying, ‘This is the way, walk in it.’” — Book of Isaiah 30:21

“For the earnest expectation of the creation eagerly waits for the revealing of the sons of God.” — Epistle to the Romans 8:19

 

Creation is waiting for the manifestation of the sons of God.

 

What Does It Mean to Manifest the Kingdom?

To manifest means:

  • To show forth

  • To make visible

  • To bring into reality

 

The manifestation of God’s Kingdom happens through believers.

 

There must be no contradiction between:

  • What the Word of God says

  • What we display in our daily lives

 

God is not a man that He should lie (Book of Numbers 23:19).
What He promises, He performs (Book of Jeremiah 32:27).

 

Sons of God: Led by the Spirit

“For as many as are led by the Spirit of God, these are sons of God.” — Romans 8:14

“It is the Spirit who gives life; the flesh profits nothing. The words that I speak to you are spirit, and they are life.” — Gospel of John 6:63

 

To be led by the Spirit is to:

  • Believe the Word

  • Act upon the Word

  • Speak the Word in every situation

 

The Lord taught us to pray:

“Let Your Kingdom come, let Your will be done on earth as it is in Heaven.” — Gospel of Matthew 6:10

 

God’s will is His Word. When we pray the Word, we pray His will.

 

The Kingdom of God Is Within You

“The Kingdom of God is within you.” — Luke 17:21

 

The power to manifest is already within you.

  • The Spirit works in you (Epistle to the Philippians 2:13)

  • The same Spirit that raised Christ lives in you (Romans 8:11)

  • You have an anointing (First Epistle of John 2:20)

  • Greater is He who is in you (1 John 4:4)

  • You have authority (Gospel of Luke 10:19)

 

How Do We Manifest the Kingdom in 2026?

1. Through Our Mindset

“For as he thinks in his heart, so is he.” — Book of Proverbs 23:7

Kingdom manifestation begins in the heart and mind.
Transformation precedes manifestation.

 

2. By Speaking the Word with Boldness

Faith speaks.
Boldness releases Heaven’s power.

“Have faith in God…” — Gospel of Mark 11:22–24

The Word must precede the manifestation (Mark 16:20).

 

3. By Producing Fruit

The manifestation of the Kingdom is fruit.

God is looking for fruit in the lives of His children (Luke 13:6–9).
Miracles testify that the Father is at work (John 10:38).
Signs confirm that the Kingdom is active (Luke 7:22–23).

 

The Truth of 2026

  • The Kingdom of God is within you.

  • The Spirit of God is leading you.

  • The Word of God is alive in you.

  • The power to manifest is already inside you.

 

This is the year to:

  • Draw closer to God

  • Listen carefully to His voice

  • Speak His Word boldly

  • Walk in obedience

  • Expect miracles

 

Creation is waiting for your revealing.

Blessed be the Name of the Lord.

Pst Dr Nellie Shani

fields.title: Inside BBI New Digital Season
fields.summary: BBI ministry’s full transition to online platforms has expanded global reach through Zoom teachings, counseling, and group deliverance clinics since 2024.
fields.body:
THE Bible reminds us in Ecclesiastes 3:1:

“To everything there is a season, and a time to every purpose under heaven.”

 

For BBI, that season arrived when Founder and CEO Nellie Shani announced a major shift in the ministry’s direction during a worship night held on March 22, 2024.

 

After years of operating from Apex Court offices, BBI is now transitioning fully into an online ministry model.

 

“BBI is standing at the door of a new season as we grow and expand,” Pastor Nellie shared with members.

 

From Physical Office to Global Online Reach

 

For seven years, BBI operated from its Apex Court location, serving believers through in-person teachings, counseling, and deliverance sessions.

 

However, as Pastor Nellie explained:

“The need for our ministry in the body of Christ has grown exponentially.”

 

To meet this growing demand, BBI is fully migrating online.

 

Beginning at the end of April 2024:

  • All teachings will be conducted via Zoom

  • Counseling sessions will be virtual

  • Deliverance sessions will be hosted online

  • Morning prayer meetings will continue uninterrupted

 

This shift allows BBI to expand beyond geographical limitations and reach believers globally.

 

Diaspora Group Deliverance Clinics Growing Rapidly

 

The transition has already begun.

 

BBI recently launched diaspora group deliverance clinics, with 67 participants attending a recent session.

 

“It was an encouragement that God is moving us in the right direction,” Pastor Nellie said.

 

The online format enables greater participation from believers across Africa, the United States, the United Kingdom, Asia, and Eastern Europe—regions where Pastor Nellie has ministered over the years.

A Ministry Built on Deliverance and Spiritual Warfare

 

Founded 15 years ago, BBI is a non-profit Christian organization focused on:

  • Spiritual warfare training

  • Deliverance ministry

  • Equipping believers to live victoriously

  • Helping Christians fulfill their God-given purpose

 

BBI provides structured group sessions and support groups for men and women, meeting twice monthly after completing deliverance training.

 

Its core mission: to see the children of God set free.

Pastor Nellie Shani: Author, Speaker, Global Minister

 

Nellie Shani is an international conference speaker and workshop leader who has ministered across multiple continents.

 

She has lived in:

  • Zambia

  • Mauritania

  • Senegal

  • The United States

  • The United Kingdom

 

Over two decades, she says the Holy Spirit trained her in spiritual warfare and deliverance while serving abroad.

 

She has authored twelve books in her Spiritual Warfare series, including:

  • Breaking Invisible Barrier – The Power of the Cross

  • Breaking Financial Curses

  • Can Witchcraft Affect A Christian

  • Step into the Supernatural

  • Live the Abundant Life

 

In a previous interview, Pastor Nellie shared that she was inspired to found BBI after recognizing “patterns of failure not only in my family line but also in the family lines of Christians around me.”

Gratitude to Long-Standing Members

 

During the announcement, Pastor Nellie expressed gratitude to those who have faithfully supported BBI through the years.

 

“We have had many committed servants of God who have stood with me and we have co-laboured as we carried the Lord’s burden together.”

 

She especially acknowledged members who relinquished official positions yet remained actively connected and supportive of the ministry.

 

Many members testified that they first learned about BBI through referrals from friends and relatives—demonstrating the ministry’s growing grassroots impact.

Why the Shift to Online Ministry 

 

The move online represents:

  • Greater global reach

  • Increased accessibility

  • Expanded group deliverance capacity

  • Continued teaching and discipleship

  • Flexibility for diaspora communities

 

In today’s digital age, online ministry allows BBI to serve far beyond the limits of physical location.

 

As Pastor Nellie emphasized, this is not a reduction—it is an expansion.

Prayer for the New Season

 

Pastor Nellie called on members to pray:

“Please pray with us as we go through the logistics of managing this new season and as we grow and expand.”

 

The transition marks not an ending, but the beginning of a broader global impact.

 

There is indeed a season for everything. For BBI, the new season is digital, global, and expanding.

 

With deliverance clinics already drawing strong participation and Zoom platforms ready to host teachings, BBI is positioned to reach more believers than ever before.

 

The mission remains the same:
Equipping believers in Jesus Christ to walk in freedom and fulfill their God-given destiny.

fields.title: BBI Launches Solomon Project
fields.summary: Join BBI Solomon Project in Nairobi. Support a lasting centre for worship, discipleship, deliverance, and spiritual transformation.
fields.body:
Breaking Barriers International (BBI) proudly announces the launch of the BBI Solomon Project, a faith-driven initiative to acquire a permanent ministry building in Nairobi, Kenya.

 

This transformative project marks a historic milestone in BBI’s mission to strengthen its foundation, expand its impact, and establish a lasting legacy for generations of believers.

 

For over 15 years, BBI has been a trusted pillar of spiritual transformation, deliverance, and discipleship, touching lives locally and globally. Even after transitioning fully online in 2024, the ministry’s growth continues to highlight the urgent need for a stable physical centre to host its expanding activities.

 

The envisioned facility will serve as a hub for worship services, discipleship training, group deliverance sessions, community outreach programs, and ministry administration. Beyond bricks and mortar, this centre will be a beacon of faith, hope, and spiritual transformation, empowering individuals and communities alike.

 

BBI warmly invites partners, builders, and kingdom collaborators to join this generational vision. Registration is now open for all who desire to contribute, support, and participate in establishing a permanent altar for God’s work in Nairobi and beyond. Together, we can secure a lasting spiritual legacy that will impact lives for decades to come.

fields.title: Let's All Be Students of Deliverance in 2026
fields.summary: BBI is calling believers worldwide to become intentional students of deliverance and spiritual warfare in 2026, grounding themselves in Scripture and practical training to stand strong in spiritual battles.
fields.body:
DRAWING from Psalms 18: 31-43, believers are being reminded that God Himself trains our hands for battle and strengthens our feet for war. Scripture declares that He makes our feet like the feet of a deer and enables us to stand on the heights.

 

Just as mountain deer are sure-footed and stable on dangerous terrain, God equips His people with spiritual discernment and knowledge. He arms our minds, sharpens our understanding, and encourages us to advance confidently into spiritual warfare. We are warriors. We are battle axes in the hand of the Lord.

 

Let us be students of deliverance by reading BBI materials, watching and listening to BBI teachings on YouTube and Facebook, and attending BBI monthly teaching gatherings and School of Ministry.

 

“We are proud of the quality of our trainees who are committed and putting into practice what they have learnt. Most of them are taking up leadership roles in their churches and communities including digital spaces,” said Irene Mbithe, Director of BBI School of Ministry.

 

She noted that the last quarter drew trainees from the United States, India, Dubai, Zambia, and Malawi, with nearly all completing the program.

 

The call is clear- let us be students of spiritual warfare

 

To enroll, partner or learn more - email:
breakingbarrierzinternational@gmail.com

fields.title: BBI Monthly Teaching : Walking in Divine Health
fields.summary: BBI hosted Pastor Dr. Nellie Shani for a powerful teaching on healing as a finished work. Discover how to shift from asking to receiving.
fields.body:
NAIROBI, Kenya — On February 28, 2026, Breaking Barriers International (BBI) hosted its monthly teaching meeting, featuring a transformative session by Pastor Dr. Nellie Shani. The online gathering, titled "Walking in Divine Health," drew over 110 participants eager to understand the spiritual mechanics of healing.

Dr. Shani challenged traditional views on prayer and sickness, emphasizing that healing is a finished work of Christ. "God is not going to do what He already did," she noted, pointing to the sacrifice on the cross as the ultimate source of health.

The session focused on shifting from "begging" God for healing to "receiving" it by faith, using the biblical example of the withered fig tree to illustrate the power of spoken words and unwavering belief.

Click on this link for a related post https://bb-international.org/childrens-bread/divine-health-5-spiritual-barriers-and-breakthroughs/

The teaching resonated deeply with the global audience, as Dr. Shani dismantled common misconceptions—such as the idea that God uses sickness to teach lessons—and provided a roadmap for overcoming spiritual roadblocks to physical restoration.

fields.text: Amish Behal shares his journey of faith, highlighting a transformative experience of being saved by Jesus during high school. Despite facing multiple setbacks and temptations from the devil, he has remained steadfast in his faith for the past six years, emphasizing the importance of choices and their consequences.

fields.title: The Birth of the Ministry
fields.body:
A 20-year journey in the school of the Holy Spirit gave birth to the BBI Ministry. In the area of deliverance the first thing that the Holy Spirit taught the founder, Pastor Nellie Shani, was about generational curses and how the sins of the forefathers could still affect us today.

Since then the founder has been involved in teaching around the world. The first meeting before BBI was formally registered took place at her residence, but later as the numbers grew she looked for a hall she could hire.

Ten days later we had an executive committee and a registered ministry called BBI in 2012. Since then God has continued to add people who work with her and share the vision, and many volunteers and partners have put their hand to the plough.

 

Objectives

  1. To teach Biblical truths concerning spiritual warfare and deliverance.
  2. To help believers in Jesus Christ identify and break generational curses.
  3. To enable believers in Jesus Christ know, live and sustain their deliverance.
  4. To counsel believers in Jesus Christ who may need specialised help beyond the teaching and prayer seminars.
  5. To identify and train workers in spiritual warfare and deliverance ministry.

fields.title: Children's Bread
fields.headline: How to pray
fields.summary: Prayer is communicating with God through praise, confession, thanksgiving, and requests, trusting His will and praying in faith. If you want it even shorter or more Scripture-styled, just say the word.
fields.content:
Prayer is simply talking to God with honesty and faith. It doesn’t require perfect words—just a sincere heart.

  1. Begin with worship
    Acknowledge who God is. Thank Him, praise Him, and honor His greatness.

  2. Confess and surrender
    Humbly bring your sins, struggles, and weaknesses before God, asking for forgiveness and help.

  3. Ask with faith
    Present your needs, desires, and concerns to God, trusting Him to hear and respond according to His will.

  4. Pray God’s Word
    Use Scripture as your guide. God’s Word strengthens your prayers and aligns your heart with His truth.

  5. Listen and trust
    Take time to be still. Trust God with the outcome and remain open to His direction.

  6. Close with gratitude
    Thank God in advance, believing He is working even when you cannot see it.

fields.title: Newsline
fields.headline: RESISTING END TIMES DECEPTION
fields.summary: The Bible focuses on helping believers discern truth from deception in the context of end-time events. It emphasizes that false teachings and misleading prophecies can lead Christians astray, causing fear, confusion, or spiritual compromise
fields.content:
The bible tells us that believers discern truth from deception in the context of end-time events. It emphasizes that false teachings and misleading prophecies can lead Christians astray, causing fear, confusion, or spiritual compromise. Key points include:

  1. Awareness of Deception: Many end-times teachings are distorted, mixing truth with falsehood to manipulate believers. Awareness and vigilance are crucial.

  2. Grounding in Scripture: The Bible is the ultimate authority. Knowledge of Scripture equips believers to identify errors and test prophecies or claims against God’s Word.

  3. Spiritual Discernment: The Holy Spirit enables Christians to distinguish truth from lies. Prayer, obedience, and a relationship with God strengthen discernment.

  4. Avoiding Fear and Panic: End-time deception often preys on fear. Believers are encouraged to remain steadfast, trusting God’s sovereignty rather than succumbing to sensationalism.

  5. Living Faithfully Now: Instead of being consumed by speculation, Christians are called to live holy, obedient, and mission-focused lives while waiting for Christ’s return.

Core Message: Believers must anchor themselves in Scripture, cultivate spiritual discernment, and resist being led astray by fear, false teachers, or sensationalized interpretations of end-time events.

fields.title: Word of Truth
fields.headline: Passing On the Spiritual Legacy
fields.summary: The message emphasizes the importance of passing on a spiritual legacy to the next generation. Just as Moses discipled Joshua to lead Israel faithfully, believers today must teach their children and others about God’s works and the gospel of Jesus Christ. The Bible warns that failing to pass on this spiritual heritage can result in future generations who “know neither the Lord nor what He has done” (Judges 2:10). Christians are encouraged to intentionally share their faith, ensuring the gospel is remembered and lived by those who come after us (1 Corinthians 15:3-5).
fields.content:
The Bible tells us that after the death of Moses, Joshua took over and was the one who led the children of Israel into Canaan, the Promised Land. 
Moses was careful to disciple his aide, Joshua, “In the ways of the Lord”. Just before Moses died, God told Moses to pass on his spiritual mantle to Joshua. 
“Now Joshua son of Nun was filled with the spirit of wisdom because Moses had laid his hands on him. So the Israelites listened to him and did what the LORD had commanded Moses” – Deuteronomy 34:9. 
God wanted a spiritual legacy passed on from Moses to the next leader, Joshua.
 However after the death of Joshua the Israelites followed God for a little while then forsook Him! We are told, 
“After that whole generation had been gathered to their ancestors, another generation grew up who knew neither the Lord nor what he had done for Israel” – Judges 2:10. 
Unfortunately there was a generation that did not tell the next generation about the God of Israel and what He had done for their nation! This is what God was avoiding when He told the Israelites in Deuteronomy 6:1-9, 
“These are the commands the Lord your God commanded me to teach you…so that your children and the children after them may fear the Lord your God as long as you live…Impress them on your children. Talk about them when you sit at home and when you walk along the road, when you lie down and when you get up…Write them on the doorframes of your houses and on your gates.” 
This was the way to pass on the spiritual legacy of Israel to the next generation. Unfortunately the Israelites failed to do this!

It is very easy to see how the children and grandchildren of a great man or woman of God can totally turn away from God because the “mantle” of faith in Jesus Christ was never passed down to them by their parents! 
If we as believers in Jesus Christ do not pass on our Christian heritage to our children, we risk having a generation after we have gone, who will know neither the Lord, nor what He has done for us! How sad that would be!


 “For what I received I passed on to you as of first importance: that Christ died for our sins according to the Scriptures, that he was buried, that he was raised on the third day according to the Scriptures” – 1 Corinthians 15:3-5.
 Prayer Points:
 1). Ask the Lord to forgive you where you have not helped pass on the Word of God to the next generation.
 2). Ask the Holy Spirit to give you opportunities where you can share the gospel with the next generation.
 3). Thank the Lord that He has called us as co-laborers with Him in spreading the Gospel.

fields.title: How God Wrote Your Life and Purpose
fields.summary: God wrote your book of destiny before you were born. Discover His plan, calling, and purpose for your life through His Word and guidance.
fields.body:
God is an Author who has written many books, but the most significant book He wrote concerns His Son, Jesus Christ.

“Therefore when Christ came into the world, he said,… 'As is written of me in the scroll, Behold, I come to do your will, O God.’”
(Hebrews 10:5,7)

Jesus came to fulfill what was already written about Him in this divine book. He could do nothing outside His book of destiny.

Even the Old Testament prophets did not fully understand that they were prophesying about Jesus. They were given a “sneak preview” of what God had already written in His eternal plan.

Jesus’ Book Was Written Before the World Began

I believe that God wrote Jesus’ book at the foundation of the world. Revelation 13:8 describes Him as:

“…The Lamb that was slain from the foundation of the world.”

If Jesus was destined to be slain from the foundation of the world, then His book of destiny had already been written, including His crucifixion.

Psalm 139:16 confirms:

“…All the days ordained for me were written in your book before one of them came to be.”

Even Jesus’ birth was foretold:

  • Isaiah 7:14: “…The virgin will conceive and give birth to a son, and will call him Immanuel.”

  • Micah 5:2: Prophesied the exact town of His birth—Bethlehem.

His crucifixion was foretold in Psalm 22:15-18, where David described in detail what would happen to Him.

Your Book Was Written Too

Just as God wrote a book about Jesus, He has written a book about you—your destiny, purpose, and calling.

Sometimes, God reveals what He has written in our book through prophecy. Jeremiah 29:11 reminds us:

“For I know the plans I have for you, says the Lord. Plans for welfare and not for evil, to give you a future and a hope.”

These plans are already written in your book. Everything in your book aligns with the Word of God. The difference between books is our area of calling.

Jeremiah 1:5 says:

“Before I formed you in the womb I knew you, before you were born I set you apart; I appointed you as a prophet to the nations.”

Isaiah 49:1 adds:

“…Before I was born the Lord called me; from my birth He made mention of my name.”

Ask God: What have You set me apart for? Who have You appointed me to be?

Prayer Points

  1. Repentance – Ask the Lord for forgiveness for living according to your own plans instead of following your book of destiny.

  2. Revelation – Ask the Holy Spirit to reveal your calling, purpose, and what God has written in your book.

  3. Thanksgiving – Thank God for planning your life before you were born and for His perfect guidance.

fields.title: Resist the Devil: How To Win Spiritual Battles
fields.summary: Learn how to resist the devil, enforce Christ’s victory, and defend your faith, family, and calling using the Word of God.
fields.author_name: Pst Dr Nellie Shani | Founder, CEO
fields.body:
IMAGINE visiting a zoo and seeing a notice that reads:

“Simba the lion has escaped from his cage, but do not be alarmed. If you leave him alone, he will also leave you alone.”

No one would take such a notice seriously. Most people would immediately run for the nearest exit.

So why do many believers live as though this were true about Satan?

The Bible’s Warning About the Enemy

Scripture paints a very different picture of the devil:

“Be self-controlled and alert. Your enemy the devil prowls around like a roaring lion looking for someone to devour.”
(1 Peter 5:8)

This is not a casual warning. Satan is described as active, intentional, and dangerous—constantly looking for opportunities to steal, kill, and destroy.

But the instruction does not stop there.

“Resist him, standing firm in the faith.”
(1 Peter 5:9)

The Bible does not tell believers to run, hide, negotiate, or ignore the enemy.
It commands us to stand and resist.

Why Believers Must Stand and Resist

In every area where Satan opposes us—our faith, health, family, calling, identity, or destiny—the only biblical response is resistance.

  • Silence gives him space

  • Passivity gives him permission

  • Avoidance gives him ground

Spiritual warfare is not optional for the believer.

Although Jesus Christ has already won the ultimate victory through His death and resurrection, we are still called to enforce that victory daily. The war is won—but battles remain.

The Only Offensive Weapon God Has Given Us

The Bible is very clear about the armour of God:

“Take…the sword of the Spirit, which is the Word of God.”
(Ephesians 6:17)

Every other piece of armour is defensive.
The Word of God is our only offensive weapon.

It is how we:

  • Confront lies

  • Resist temptation

  • Overcome fear

  • Silence accusation

  • Push back darkness

Jesus Showed Us How to Fight

When Satan tempted Jesus in the wilderness, Jesus did not panic, argue, or retreat.

Every response began with the same phrase:

“It is written…”

Jesus resisted Satan using the Word of God—and Satan fled.

If Jesus, the Son of God, used Scripture as His weapon, how much more should we?

What Satan Is Trying to Steal

Jesus clearly revealed the enemy’s agenda:

“The thief comes only to steal and kill and destroy.”
(John 10:10)

Satan seeks to:

  • Kill through sickness, accidents, calamities, murder, and abortion

  • Steal dignity, marriages, relationships, reputation, health, wealth, and truth through deception and false doctrine

  • Destroy hope, faith, peace, joy, and purpose

He targets what Jesus has already given us:

  • Righteousness

  • Healing

  • Blessing

  • Provision

  • Authority

We are not called to watch helplessly while these things are taken.
We are called to resist.

Authority Has Already Been Given

Jesus declared plainly:

“I have given you authority to trample on snakes and scorpions and to overcome all the power of the enemy; nothing will harm you.”
(Luke 10:19)

The issue is not lack of authority.
The issue is failure to use it.

When believers stand firm, speak the Word of God, and resist the devil, he flees.

“In all these things we are more than conquerors through Him who loved us.”
(Romans 8:37)

A Personal Question

What has Satan tried to take from you?

  • Your peace?

  • Your health?

  • Your family?

  • Your calling?

  • Your joy?

Are you going to let him get away with it—or will you take your stand?

Wield your sword.
Speak the Word.
Resist him in the name of Jesus Christ.

Prayer Points

1. Repentance

Ask God to forgive you for times you allowed the enemy to take what Jesus already secured for you by failing to resist him.

2. Strength and Boldness

Ask the Holy Spirit to help you stand firm and consistently use the Word of God whenever the enemy opposes you.

3. Thanksgiving

Thank the Lord that you have authority over Satan and that He who is in you is greater than he who is in the world.

fields.title: You Are Not a Worm | Understanding Your Worth in God’s Eyes
fields.summary: Many misunderstand humility as self-belittling. Scripture reveals your true worth, identity, and value as one created in God’s image.
fields.author_name: Pst Dr Nellie Shani | Founder, CEO
fields.body:
MANY believers misunderstand humility. We often assume that being humble means belittling ourselves, speaking poorly about our worth, or seeing ourselves as insignificant. However, the Bible presents a very different picture of humility—one that is rooted in truth, identity, and honor before God.

I once heard someone pray:

“Lord, have mercy on us. We are just worms in Your sight.”

This raises an important question:
Would God send His only beloved Son to die a cruel death on the cross for worms?
Of course not.

Tragically, some people have even taken their own lives because they felt worthless and unloved, believing their lives had no value. Scripture tells us the opposite. You are far more valuable than you realize.

God Created You in His Image

The Bible clearly reveals humanity’s worth:

“Then God said, ‘Let us make mankind in our image, in our likeness…’”
(Genesis 1:26)

God created human beings in His image and likeness, granting them authority, responsibility, and a unique place in creation. Humanity was designed not only to rule but to walk in fellowship with God.

“Then the man and his wife heard the sound of the Lord God as He was walking in the garden in the cool of the day.”
(Genesis 3:8)

Imagine the Creator of the universe seeking fellowship with human beings. That alone reveals how deeply God values relationship with us.

God’s Plan to Restore Fellowship With Humanity

When sin entered the world, God did not abandon humanity. Instead, He immediately set in motion a redemptive plan.

God placed the weight of humanity’s sin upon His Son, Jesus Christ, so that fellowship could be restored. Through the cross, forgiveness was made available, and humanity was reconciled back to God.

This sacrifice is the ultimate proof of your value. You were worth redeeming.

You Are the Apple of God’s Eye

King David, a man after God’s own heart, prayed:

“Keep me as the apple of your eye; hide me in the shadow of your wings.”
(Psalm 17:8)

Every believer in Jesus Christ is precious in God’s sight. He watches over us, protects us, and draws us close—not because we are insignificant, but because we are deeply loved.

You Are Not Worthless—You Are Loved

If you have ever felt forgotten, insignificant, or cast aside, Scripture speaks directly to you.

“He who did not spare His own Son, but gave Him up for us all—how will He not also, along with Him, graciously give us all things?”
(Romans 8:32)

fields.title: Overcoming Fear of Satan | Spiritual Warfare For Every Believer
fields.summary: Learn how to overcome fear of Satan, resist the enemy, and walk in authority using the Word of God. Stand firm and experience victory in Christ today.
fields.author_name: Pst Dr Nellie Shani | Founder, CEO
fields.body:
MANY believers fear Satan and spiritual warfare, yet Scripture teaches us to stand firm, use the Word of God, and walk in authority and victory in Christ.

One reason fear persists is that spiritual warfare is rarely taught from pulpits. Most sermons focus on salvation, hope, faith, and love—which are vital—but the topic of the devil and spiritual warfare is often avoided.

Some ministers avoid it to prevent fear in their congregations or to avoid giving attention to Satan. While well-intentioned, this often produces the opposite effect: ignorance breeds fear.

Fear Often Comes from a Lack of Knowledge

Many believers’ understanding of Satan is shaped by culture, cartoons, or exaggerated stories—none of which are biblical.

Even after coming to Christ, fear can remain. Without personal study of the Word of God, false images and intimidation persist. Sadly, this is still true for many believers today.

Every Believer Is Already in Spiritual Warfare

Whether we acknowledge it or not, every believer is part of the Lord Jesus Christ’s army.

Spiritual warfare is not optional. Ignoring it leads to unnecessary defeat, harassment, and vulnerability to the enemy’s schemes. Scripture warns us clearly about the adversary—pretending he doesn’t exist does not remove him.

Victory depends on understanding three key truths:

  1. Who Jesus Christ is

  2. Who we are in Christ

  3. Who Satan really is

The Truth About Satan’s Defeat

The Bible reassures us:

“He who is in you is greater than he who is in the world.”
(1 John 4:4)

Through His death and resurrection, Jesus Christ defeated Satan:

“And having disarmed the powers and authorities, He made a public spectacle of them, triumphing over them by the cross.”
(Colossians 2:15)

Satan is not all-powerful. His influence lies in deception and intimidation, not real authority.

Authority Has Been Given to the Believer

Jesus not only defeated Satan, He delegated authority to believers.

We are not to fear the devil, but we are also not to ignore him. Scripture instructs us to resist him—and resisting requires action and knowledge of the Word of God.

“God raised us up with Christ and seated us with Him in the heavenly realms in Christ Jesus.”
(Ephesians 2:6)

This is our position: authority, victory, and confidence in Christ.

From Fear to Confidence in Christ

Many believers fear Satan because they do not yet know Scripture well enough to see his defeat.

Growing in God’s Word replaces fear with revelation, and intimidation with confidence.

“I have given you authority to trample on snakes and scorpions and to overcome all the power of the enemy; nothing will harm you.”
(Luke 10:19)

Believers are on the winning side. We have authority. We have victory.

Prayer Points

1. Repentance

Ask God to forgive you for fearing Satan and for not studying the Word enough to know that he is defeated.

2. Revelation

Pray that the Holy Spirit reveals Jesus Christ as the Lion of the tribe of Judah, supreme over all powers of darkness.

3. Thanksgiving

Thank the Lord that you are on the winning side and that He has given you authority over all the power of the enemy.

fields.title: Walking in God’s Work and Mission Today
fields.summary: Learn how to join God in His work by following Jesus’ example. Walk in obedience, discern His will, and serve faithfully in every area of life.
fields.author_name: Pst Dr Nellie Shani | Founder, CEO
fields.body:
IMAGINE the anxiety Jesus’ disciples must have felt as they watched Him ascend into the sky, disappearing until a cloud hid Him from their sight.

For over three years, they had followed Him faithfully, participating in the work God the Father had given Jesus to do. Even in His final moments on earth, Jesus remained focused on His mission, never allowing distractions to pull Him off course.

Jesus’ Mission Was Always Aligned with the Father

Jesus did not act on His own will. He said:

“My Father is always at His work to this very day, and I too am working.”
(John 5:17)

What God the Father was accomplishing in the invisible spiritual realm, Jesus manifested visibly on earth.

“…The Son can do nothing by himself; he can do only what he sees his Father doing, because whatever the Father does, the Son also does.”
(John 5:19)

Jesus never allowed distractions to divert Him from the work God had called Him to do. To understand the Father’s work, we only need to observe the life of Jesus:

“For the Father loves the Son and shows him all He does…”
(John 5:20)

The Father’s Work Revealed Through Jesus

Who raised Lazarus, the widow’s son at Nain, or Jairus’ daughter? It was God the Father, working through Jesus.

“For just as the Father raises the dead and gives them life, even so the Son gives life to whom he is pleased to give it.”
(John 5:21)

Jesus’ earthly mission culminated on the cross:

“I have brought you glory on earth by finishing the work you gave me to do.”
(John 17:4)

Notice: He said the work given to Him was finished, not all work on earth. The Father’s work continues today—through His children.

Joining God in His Work Today

God is still active in the world—saving souls, healing the sick, and delivering those in bondage. He invites us to join Him, just as Jesus did.

To participate, we must walk closely with the Father, following Jesus’ example:

  • Read the Bible regularly

  • Pray without ceasing

  • Seek God’s guidance before acting

“As the Father has sent me, so I am sending you.”
(John 20:21)

Where is God at work in your life—family, office, business, or hospital? Where is there fruit (Galatians 5:22)? That is where He is inviting you to participate.

Lessons from Jesus’ Example

  1. Do not run your own show.

  2. Observe what God is doing and join Him.

  3. Walk in obedience and faith, following Jesus’ example.

Prayer Points

  • Repentance: Ask the Father to forgive you for acting on your own instead of seeking His will.

  • Revelation: Ask the Holy Spirit to enlighten your spiritual eyes, revealing what the Father is doing.

  • Thanksgiving: Thank Jesus for leaving us an example of how to walk closely with the Father

fields.title: Why Young Believers Need God’s Undiluted Word
fields.summary: Entertainment alone cannot grow spiritual maturity. Discover why teaching young believers the pure, undiluted Word of God is essential for lasting faith, growth, and victory in Christ.
fields.author_name: Pst Dr Nellie Shani | Founder, CEO
fields.body:
The Danger of Entertainment Without Discipleship

UNFORTUNATELY, much singing, dancing, and constant entertainment—combined with very little teaching of the Word—has produced young believers who must continually be entertained in order to stay engaged. While joyful worship has its place, it can never replace the foundational role of Scripture in a believer’s life.

Funny stories and personal spiritual experiences may amuse a congregation, but they cannot feed a hungry soul. Only the pure Word of God has the power to produce real spiritual growth.

“For the word of God is alive and active. Sharper than any double-edged sword…” – Hebrews 4:12

Following Jesus Means Knowing His Word

Those who choose to follow Jesus Christ must be taught how to truly follow Him. Jesus is revealed through His Word. Without Scripture, believers are left without direction, depth, or endurance.

“Study to show yourself approved unto God, a workman that needs not to be ashamed, rightly dividing the word of truth.” – 2 Timothy 2:15

Believers must learn to rightly divide the Word of truth in order to walk in victory. Any ministry that encourages people to study the Bible for themselves will bear lasting fruit.

Shallow Teaching Produces Shallow Roots

Very little grows in shallow soil. Jesus Himself warned about this when He spoke of seed sown on rocky ground:

“Some fell on rocky places, where it did not have much soil. It sprang up quickly, because the soil was shallow. But when the sun came up, the plants were scorched, and they withered because they had no root.” – Matthew 13:5–6

A shallow or diluted message may produce excitement, but it cannot sustain faith when challenges arise.

Simple Is Not the Same as Diluted

No believer is too young to understand the Word of God. Giving young believers a diluted message will not produce a transformed life. There is a vital difference between a simple Word and a diluted Word.

A simple mango juice is made purely from mango.
A diluted mango juice has water added to it.

The same is true of Scripture. God’s Word can be taught simply—without watering it down.

If someone understood the gospel well enough to choose to follow Jesus Christ, then the Holy Spirit, who now lives within them, will help them understand the Word of God.

“The seed falling on rocky ground refers to someone who hears the word and at once receives it with joy. But since they have no root, they last only a short time.” – Matthew 13:20–21

The Path to Spiritual Maturity

Sadly, many young believers who are not grounded in the Word fall away when trouble or persecution comes because of their faith. Spiritual maturity only develops through consistent engagement with Scripture—reading it, meditating on it, and obeying it.

“But solid food is for the mature, who by constant use have trained themselves to distinguish good from evil.” – Hebrews 5:14

A strong faith is built on a strong foundation, and that foundation is the Word of God.

Prayer Points

  1. Repentance – Ask the Lord for forgiveness for not maturing in your faith due to inconsistency in His Word.

  2. Discipleship – Ask the Holy Spirit to bring young believers into your life with whom you can share the undiluted Word of God.

  3. Thanksgiving – Thank the Father for His Word, which is living, active, and sharper than any two-edged sword.

fields.title: Why Some Believers Fall Away
fields.summary: Why do some believers fall away from Christ? Discover biblical insights from the Parable of the Sower, Matthew 24:13, and Psalm 1 on spiritual maturity, worldly influence, and how to stand firm to the end.
fields.body:
Why Do Some Believers Start Strong — But Fail to Finish?

IT is often said that many African American singers began in church choirs before transitioning into secular music. Beyond music, we also see many people living lifestyles completely contrary to biblical values who once professed faith in Jesus Christ.

This raises an important spiritual question:

Why do some believers start strong in their faith but later drift away?

The Bible reminds us that salvation is not merely about how we begin — but how we finish.

“But the one who stands firm to the end will be saved.” – Matthew 24:13

Spiritual endurance matters.

The Parable of the Sower: Seed Among Thorns

In Luke 8:7, Jesus describes seed that fell among thorns:

“Other seed fell among thorns, which grew up with it and choked the plants.”

Later, He explains the meaning in Luke 8:14:

“The seed that fell among thorns stands for those who hear, but as they go on their way they are choked by life’s worries, riches and pleasures, and they do not mature.”

This passage describes believers who receive the Word of God but never grow to spiritual maturity.

Why?

Because they grow among thorns.

What Are the “Thorns” That Choke Spiritual Growth?

Jesus identifies three powerful thorns:

  1. Life’s worries

  2. Riches

  3. Pleasures

When believers surround themselves with people who pursue wealth, fame, status, and worldly success above all else, their spiritual sensitivity gradually dulls.

They are constantly exposed to:

  • Anxiety about the future

  • Obsession with material gain

  • Conversations rooted in hopelessness

  • Values contrary to God’s Kingdom

Jesus warned:

“For the pagans run after all these things…” – Matthew 6:32

It is difficult to mature spiritually while constantly immersed in environments that glorify what God warns against.

As the saying goes:

“Show me your company, and I will tell you your character.”

Can We Mature Spiritually While Growing Among Thorns?

The honest biblical answer is no.

Spiritual maturity requires intentional separation in values — not isolation from people, but separation from systems that oppose God.

Psalm 1:1–2 gives us the blueprint for standing firm:

“Blessed is the one who does not walk in step with the wicked or stand in the way that sinners take or sit in the company of mockers, but whose delight is in the law of the Lord, and who meditates on his law day and night.”

The result?

“That person is like a tree planted by streams of water… whose leaf does not wither. Whatever they do prospers.”

This is the secret to endurance:

  • Guard your influences

  • Delight in God’s Word

  • Meditate daily

  • Choose company wisely

How Do We Be “Salt and Light” Without Compromise?

A common concern is:

If we avoid worldly influence, how can we reach people?

Jesus Christ provides the perfect example.

He interacted with tax collectors, prostitutes, and sinners — but He did not adopt their values or seek their fellowship for companionship.

“…What fellowship can light have with darkness?” – 2 Corinthians 6:14

Jesus engaged sinners with purpose — not for validation, entertainment, or identity.

When religious leaders criticized Him, He responded:

“…Truly I tell you, the tax collectors and the prostitutes are entering the kingdom of God ahead of you.” – Matthew 21:31

He influenced them.
They did not influence Him.

Salt Changes the Food — Not the Other Way Around

When salt is added to food, the salt changes the flavor. The food does not change the salt.

Likewise:

When believers are around non-believers:

  • Our character should remain intact

  • Our values should remain stable

  • Our light should shine

Jesus said:

“Let your light so shine before men, that they may see your good works and give glory to your Father who is in heaven.” – Matthew 5:16

We are called to influence — not absorb.

Why Some Believers Drift Away

Many believers fall away because:

  • They grow among thorns

  • They absorb worldly value systems

  • They prioritize riches over righteousness

  • They neglect meditation on God’s Word

  • They allow relationships to reshape their convictions

“Birds of a feather flock together.”

Influence is powerful. Over time, values shift subtly — then permanently.

The Key to Standing Firm to the End

Spiritual endurance requires:

  • Guarding your associations

  • Prioritizing Scripture daily

  • Remaining rooted in Christ

  • Engaging the world with purpose — not compromise

Finishing strong is intentional.

It does not happen accidentally.

Final Encouragement

The goal is not isolation but  maturity.

Grow deeply rooted in Christ — so when you engage the world, you remain unshaken.

The one who stands firm to the end will be saved.

Prayer Points

1. Repentance
Ask the Lord to forgive you for areas where worldly influence has shaped your values more than His Word.

2. Empowerment
Ask the Holy Spirit to help you be salt and light wherever you are placed.

3. Gratitude
Thank God for calling you to be a co-laborer in bringing transformation to the world.

fields.title: How to Discover and Walk in Your Calling
fields.summary: What is the difference between spiritual gifts and the fruit of the Spirit? Learn how to bear fruit, discover your spiritual gift, and overcome the obstacles that stop believers from walking in their calling.
fields.author_name: Pst Dr Nellie Shani | Founder, CEO
fields.body:
Spiritual Gifts vs. Fruit of the Spirit: What’s the Difference?

A gift is something we receive freely.
But fruit is something that must be cultivated.

This is a crucial biblical distinction that every believer must understand.

While spiritual gifts are given by grace, the fruit of the Spirit is developed through surrender, obedience, and spiritual growth.

Jesus said:

“…While every branch that does bear fruit he prunes so that it will be even more fruitful.” – John 15:2

Pruning requires process. Growth requires cooperation. Bearing fruit takes work.

How Do We Bear the Fruit of the Spirit?

Many believers assume that the Holy Spirit automatically produces fruit in us. However, Scripture teaches that fruit must be nurtured.

When we are saved, the Holy Spirit indwells us — but the manifestation of the fruit depends on how we “water the tree.”

How do we water the Tree of the Holy Spirit?

We water it through:

  • Daily surrender

  • Obedience to the Word of God

  • Prayer

  • Repentance

  • Spiritual discipline

The fruit that God desires us to manifest is clearly listed in Galatians 5:22:

“But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faithfulness, gentleness, and self-control.”

It is God’s will that every believer bear much fruit.

Fruit reflects character.
Fruit proves maturity.
Fruit glorifies God.

Are Spiritual Gifts Only for a Few Chosen Believers?

When it comes to the fruit of the Spirit, most believers agree that all Christians are called to manifest it.

But confusion often arises regarding spiritual gifts.

Some believe that spiritual gifts are reserved for a select few. Scripture says otherwise.

When the Holy Spirit descended in the upper room, He came upon each and every believer present.

Every believer has received at least one spiritual gift to fulfill their God-given purpose.

Romans 12:6 teaches:

“We have different gifts according to the grace given us…”

The passage then lists examples of spiritual gifts:

  • Prophecy

  • Serving

  • Teaching

  • Encouraging

  • Giving

  • Leadership

  • Showing mercy

Spiritual gifts are given according to grace, not merit.

Why Many Believers Don’t Walk in Their Spiritual Gifts

Unfortunately, many Christians do not know what their spiritual gifts are. There are three major enemies that prevent believers from walking in their gifting.

1️⃣ Comparison and Jealousy

Focusing on others — how they look, what they have, or the impact they make — leads to:

  • Jealousy

  • Competition

  • Self-rejection

  • Discouragement

Comparison distracts you from your assignment.

When you focus on someone else’s calling, you neglect your own.

2️⃣ Ignorance of Your Spiritual Gift

If you do not understand your gift, you may try to run someone else’s race.

The result?

  • Frustration

  • Failure

  • Self-pity

  • Blaming God

But God is not unfair. He distributes gifts wisely and intentionally.

You were designed with purpose.

3️⃣ Lack of Prayer

Prayer is essential for discovering and developing your spiritual gift.

Without prayer:

  • You will not grow in your gift

  • You may not recognize it

  • You may neglect it

Ask the Holy Spirit to reveal your gifting so you can serve effectively in the Body of Christ.

How to Discover Your Spiritual Gift

If you’re asking, “How do I know my spiritual gift?” — here are three biblical self-evaluation questions:

1️⃣ What do you do naturally that blesses others?

What comes easily to you but impacts others deeply?

2️⃣ What brings you joy and fulfillment?

Your spiritual gift often aligns with your God-given passion.

3️⃣ What brings glory to God?

Your gift should ultimately point people to Christ — not yourself.

The answers to these three questions often reveal your spiritual gifting.

Scripture encourages clarity:

“Now about the gifts of the Spirit, brothers and sisters, I do not want you to be uninformed.” – 1 Corinthians 12:1

God does not want you confused about your calling.

Why Walking in Your Spiritual Gift Matters

Your spiritual gift:

  • Strengthens the church

  • Builds up the body of Christ

  • Advances God’s Kingdom

  • Brings personal fulfillment

  • Glorifies God

When you neglect your gift, the Body suffers loss.

When you walk in your gift, others are blessed.

The Balance: Fruit and Gifts Together

Fruit without gifts limits impact.
Gifts without fruit damage character.

God desires both:

  • Christlike character (fruit of the Spirit)

  • Spirit-empowered service (spiritual gifts)

Maturity happens when both operate together.

You are not giftless.
You are not forgotten.
You are not insignificant.

You have received grace for a purpose.

Discover your spiritual gift.Develop it in prayer.Demonstrate it with humility.
And bear fruit that remains

Prayer Points

1. Repentance for Lack of Fruit
Ask the Lord to forgive you where you have not surrendered fully to His Word, preventing spiritual growth.

2. Revelation of Your Spiritual Gift
Ask the Holy Spirit to help you answer the three discovery questions and reveal your gifting.

3. Gratitude for Your Calling
Thank God that you have been uniquely gifted to bless the Body of Christ.

fields.title: Discovering God’s Plan for Your Life
fields.summary: What is God’s plan for your life? Learn how Jeremiah 29:11, Proverbs 3:5–6, and Psalm 139 reveal your divine purpose and how to submit your decisions to God’s will.
fields.body:
God Has a Plan for Your Life — But Are You Living It?

Jeremiah 29:11 declares:

“For I know the plans I have for you…”

If God has a plan for your life, then your life is not random — and it is not self-directed.

Many people believe they have the right to:

  • Choose any career they want

  • Live wherever they want

  • Marry whoever they want

  • Make decisions independent of God

But Scripture presents a different perspective.

1 Corinthians 6:20 reminds us:

“You are not your own, for you were bought with a price.”

When you belong to God, your life is no longer self-owned. It is purpose-driven.

You Were Designed Before You Were Born

Psalm 139:15–16 reveals something powerful:

“Your eyes saw my unformed body; all the days ordained for me were written in your book before one of them came to be.”

Before you were placed in your mother’s womb:

  • God knew you

  • God wrote about you

  • God ordained your days

Your purpose was predetermined.

This explains why so many people feel frustrated and unfulfilled. They are living — but not aligned with God’s divine plan.

Why Many People Feel Lost About Their Purpose

When we make life decisions without seeking God’s direction, we risk stepping outside His design.

Proverbs 3:5–6 gives clear instruction:

“Trust in the Lord with all your heart and lean not on your own understanding; in all your ways submit to him, and he will make your paths straight.”

The key phrase is:

“In all your ways submit to Him.”

Do we truly submit to God before:

  • Accepting a job offer?

  • Moving to a new city?

  • Entering a relationship?

  • Making major life decisions?

Or do we decide first and then ask God to bless our choice?

Jeremiah’s Appointment: A Clear Example of Divine Calling

In Jeremiah 1:5, God tells Jeremiah:

“Before I formed you in the womb I knew you, before you were born I set you apart; I appointed you as a prophet to the nations.”

Jeremiah was appointed — not self-selected.

He was called to be a prophet and nothing else.

Until we discover who God appointed us to be, we will never experience deep fulfillment.

Stop Running Everywhere — Start Listening

Instead of applying to six different jobs, what if you asked:

“Lord, where have You appointed me?”

Instead of asking God to approve your plans, ask Him to reveal His plans.

True peace comes from alignment, not ambition.

It is often said:

The two most important days in your life are the day you were born and the day you discover why you were born.

Discovering your purpose requires walking closely with the Holy Spirit.

How to Hear God’s Voice and Discover Your Purpose

Isaiah 30:21 promises:

“Whether you turn to the right or to the left, your ears will hear a voice behind you, saying, ‘This is the way; walk in it.’”

But how do we become sensitive to God’s direction?

1️⃣ Read the Word Consistently

A baby recognizes its mother’s voice because it hears it constantly. Likewise, you recognize God’s voice by immersing yourself in Scripture.

2️⃣ Submit Before You Decide

Submission is not a last step — it is the first step.

3️⃣ Walk Where You Are Planted

Where God plants you is where you will bear much fruit. Alignment produces fruitfulness.

4️⃣ Pray for Direction, Not Just Blessing

Shift your prayer from:
“Bless what I’m doing”
to
“Lead me where You want me.”

Signs You Are Walking in God’s Plan

When you align with God’s purpose:

  • There is peace even in difficulty

  • Your gifts begin to flourish

  • You bear fruit naturally

  • Doors open without manipulation

  • You experience deep satisfaction

Purpose produces fulfillment.

Your Destiny Is in God’s Hands

God is not hiding your purpose from you.

He created you for a reason.

As you:

  • Trust Him

  • Submit your ways

  • Read His Word

  • Listen for His voice

He will direct your path.

The plan for your life is safest in God’s hands — not your own.

Prayer Points

1️⃣ Repentance
Ask the Lord to forgive you for leaning on your own understanding instead of seeking His plan for your life.

2️⃣ Revelation
Ask the Holy Spirit to reveal God’s purpose and direction as you continue to submit your ways to Him.

3️⃣ Gratitude
Thank the Lord that He created you intentionally and is guiding you into your destiny as you walk in obedience.

fields.title: Passing the Spiritual Mantle
fields.summary: Why must believers pass their faith to the next generation? Learn biblical lessons from Moses, Joshua, Judges 2:10, and Deuteronomy 6 on preserving a spiritual legacy.
fields.author_name: Pst Dr Nellie Shani | Founder, CEO
fields.body:
The Importance of Passing a Spiritual Legacy

THE Bible tells us that after the death of Moses, Joshua led the children of Israel into the Promised Land. Moses did not simply lead — he discipled.

He intentionally prepared Joshua “in the ways of the Lord.”

Before Moses died, God instructed him to pass on his spiritual mantle to Joshua.

“Now Joshua son of Nun was filled with the spirit of wisdom because Moses had laid his hands on him…” – Deuteronomy 34:9

This was not accidental succession.
It was intentional spiritual transfer.

God desired continuity. He wanted the faith, wisdom, and obedience that marked Moses to continue through Joshua.

What Happens When a Spiritual Legacy Is Not Passed Down?

After Joshua’s death, Israel followed God for a time. But something tragic happened:

“Another generation grew up who knew neither the Lord nor what he had done for Israel.” – Judges 2:10

How does an entire generation grow up not knowing God — after witnessing miracles, deliverance, and the Promised Land?

The answer is sobering:

They were not taught.

The spiritual mantle was not passed down.

God’s Clear Command: Teach the Next Generation

God had already warned Israel about this danger in Deuteronomy 6:1–9:

“Impress them on your children. Talk about them when you sit at home and when you walk along the road, when you lie down and when you get up…”

God commanded:

  • Teach your children diligently

  • Speak about His Word daily

  • Make faith part of everyday life

  • Write His commands visibly

Passing on faith was never meant to be occasional — it was to be intentional and constant.

Unfortunately, Israel failed to obey this command fully.

Why Children of Great Believers Sometimes Walk Away

It is easy to see how the children and grandchildren of a powerful man or woman of God can drift from the faith.

Why?

Because faith was admired — but not transferred.

The mantle of faith in Jesus Christ must be intentionally passed down through:

  • Teaching

  • Example

  • Conversation

  • Prayer

  • Personal testimony

Christian heritage is not inherited automatically. It must be nurtured.

The Danger of Spiritual Silence

If believers today do not actively pass on their faith:

We risk raising a generation who:

  • Know culture but not Christ

  • Know trends but not truth

  • Know success but not salvation

The tragedy described in Judges 2:10 can repeat itself in any generation.

What a sorrow it would be for future generations to say:

“They never told us.”

What Does It Mean to Pass the Mantle of Faith?

Passing the spiritual mantle means:

  • Sharing the gospel clearly

  • Teaching Scripture consistently

  • Modeling obedience

  • Testifying of God’s faithfulness

  • Living a visible Christian life

The Apostle Paul understood this principle:

“For what I received I passed on to you as of first importance…” – 1 Corinthians 15:3–5

Christianity has always advanced through faithful transmission.

What we receive, we must pass on.

How to Preserve a Godly Legacy Today

If we want to leave a lasting spiritual legacy:

1️⃣ Make Faith Visible at Home

Let prayer, Scripture, and worship be normal parts of daily life.

2️⃣ Share Personal Testimonies

Tell your children and the next generation what God has done for you.

3️⃣ Prioritize Discipleship

Church attendance alone is not discipleship. Intentional conversation is necessary.

4️⃣ Model Authentic Christianity

Children imitate what they see more than what they hear.

5️⃣ Share the Gospel Clearly

Ensure the next generation understands salvation through Christ.

Your Legacy Matters

Your greatest inheritance to the next generation is not money, property, or education.

It is faith.

A spiritual legacy outlives you.

Let it never be said of our generation that those who came after us “knew neither the Lord nor what He had done.”

Pass the mantle.Teach the Word.Live the faith.

Prayer Points

1️⃣ Repentance
Ask the Lord to forgive you where you have not intentionally passed on the Word of God to the next generation.

2️⃣ Opportunity
Ask the Holy Spirit to open doors for you to share the gospel with children, youth, and those coming after you.

3️⃣ Gratitude
Thank God that He has called you to be a co-laborer in spreading the Gospel and preserving a godly legacy.

fields.title: From Hearing to Transformation
fields.summary: Discover how to become “good soil” for God’s Word. Learn how hearing, retaining, and applying Scripture leads to spiritual growth, renewed minds (Romans 12:2), and lasting transformation.
fields.author_name: Pst Dr Nellie Shani | Founder, CEO
fields.body:
“Still other seed fell on good soil. It came up and yielded a crop, a hundred times more than was sown” – Luke 8:8.

IN the Parable of the Sower, Jesus teaches a powerful truth: the condition of our hearts determines whether the Word of God produces fruit in our lives. The reason we gather for church, sermons, and Bible teaching is not simply to listen—but to be transformed.

Why We Go to Church: Transformation Through God’s Word

Scripture tells us in Romans 12:2:

“Be transformed by the renewing of your mind.”

True Christian transformation happens when our minds are renewed through Scripture. Spiritual growth does not occur automatically just because we attend church. It happens when the Word of God takes root in receptive hearts.

The important question is:
How much of the Word preached every Sunday falls on good soil?

Sadly, much of it falls on distracted, tired, or disinterested hearts. Sleepiness, wandering thoughts, mobile phones, stress, and the cares of life often compete for our attention. When the soil is distracted, the seed struggles to grow.

What Is “Good Soil”?

Jesus explains in Luke 8:15:

“But the seed on good soil stands for those with a noble and good heart, who hear the word, retain it, and by persevering produce a crop.”

Good soil is marked by three things:

  1. Hearing the Word of God

  2. Retaining (remembering and meditating on) the Word

  3. Persevering until it produces fruit

Spiritual fruitfulness requires intentional listening and obedience.

How to Hear and Retain the Word of God

In a world full of distractions, we must deliberately cultivate spiritual focus. Here are practical ways to grow in hearing and retaining God’s Word:

1. Eliminate Distractions

Silence your phone. Calm your thoughts. Prepare your heart before the sermon begins. Ask the Holy Spirit to help you focus.

2. Take Notes During the Sermon

Writing key points helps improve memory and understanding. It allows the message to move from passive hearing to active engagement.

3. Share What You Learned

Repeating the message to a friend or family member strengthens retention and deepens understanding.

4. Reflect and Apply

Ask yourself:

  • What does this Scripture reveal about God?

  • What is God saying to me personally?

  • What is He asking me to change?

  • What is He asking me to start doing?

Transformation happens when we move from hearing to doing.

The Role of the Holy Spirit in Spiritual Growth

We cannot bear spiritual fruit in our own strength. We need the help of the Holy Spirit to:

  • Understand God’s Word

  • Apply it to daily life

  • Obey it faithfully

  • Persevere when it is challenging

When we allow the Spirit to work in us, the Word of God produces lasting change in our thoughts, attitudes, and actions.

A Call to Listen

Revelation 2:7 reminds us:

“Whoever has ears, let them hear what the Spirit says to the churches.”

God is always speaking. The question is not whether He is speaking—but whether we are truly listening.

When the Word of God falls on good soil, it does not return empty. It produces a harvest—changed thinking, transformed living, and visible spiritual fruit.

May we be people who not only hear the Word but retain it, obey it, and allow it to transform our lives.

Prayer Points for Spiritual Growth

  1. Repentance for Distraction
    Ask the Lord to forgive you for times when you have not listened attentively to His Word.

  2. Help to Hear and Obey
    Ask the Holy Spirit to help you honor God by giving careful attention to Scripture and obeying it fully.

  3. Gratitude for Teachers of the Word
    Thank the Father for pastors, teachers, and leaders He has called to faithfully preach and teach His Word.

fields.title: Expect Persecution but Walk in Victory
fields.summary: Discover why persecution is part of the Christian walk, how to possess all Christ has given, and walk in victory as a believer in spiritual warfare.
fields.author_name: Pst Dr Nellie Shani | Founder, CEO
fields.body:
MANY believers get flustered, frustrated, and discouraged when they encounter persecution in their Christian walk. They give their lives to Christ expecting life to be calm, peaceful, and easy.

The reality is different. The life of a believer is peaceful, but it is not always easy. Why? Because when you follow Jesus, you begin to dance to a different tune than the world. Your passions change, your pursuits shift, your speech and values transform—and the world’s systems begin to oppose you.

Your thirst for righteousness makes others uncomfortable. Ironically, it is often non-believers who hold you to the standards of your faith:

“How can you do that? I thought you said you are a believer!”

The Bible has never promised an easy life to those who follow God’s principles. In 2 Timothy 3:12, we read:
